title: ----
content:
----
Of course. Re-imagining "Infinite Wiki" as a tool for a personal knowledge base is a fascinating idea. Instead of a public-facing encyclopedia, it would become a dynamic, self-organizing "second brain" that helps you connect and explore your own thoughts.

Let's call this theoretical application "Synapse".

Here’s how the core functionalities would be adapted for a personal knowledge base:

Core Concept: From Knowledge Consumer to Idea Gardener
The fundamental shift is from consuming general knowledge to cultivating personal insights. Synapse wouldn't just define terms; it would weave together your own notes, ideas, and documents into an interconnected web. The AI's role changes from being a "generator of facts" to a "serendipity engine" and an "intellectual partner."

Adapting the Key Features
Data Source: Your Personal Corpus
Instead of calling out to the open internet for every definition, Synapse would be grounded in your personal data. This could be a local folder of Markdown files, a connection to a service like Obsidian or Notion, or its own internal database of your notes. This becomes the "source of truth."
